bullets: A wave of cheap Korean cargo — now including Sabic's S-Oil joint venture — reset PP bids sharply lower and keeps undercutting Chinese supply.
Producers keep cutting to move volume, yet domestic homo is still seen as too high to sell.
Chinese import offers stay thin and scattered as buyers hold out for the cheaper Korean material.
PE swung sharply as Chinese futures dropped and sellers wait on Sabic's July settlement to set direction.
Cheap but distant US and deeply-cut Russian cargo are dragging sentiment lower; domestic grades keep falling.
HDPE remains the firmest corner of the market on thin ready-delivery inventory.
PVC led the market lower today as domestic producer offers were cut sharply, intensifying downward pressure.
Coal-based Chinese cargo dropped further, with talk of base prices approaching the 20,000 VND/kg mark next month.
Oil-based grades are trading slowly; cheap US import cargo sits idle on its far-October arrival.

editorial.changedNote: 17 of 50 fell, led by PVC and PP

summary: Dalian LLDPE futures -30 in two days; buyers waiting on Sabic's July settlement before trading.
Sabic July CIF bids reset sharply lower; Petrochina LLDPE -10 to 1,010-1,020; US/Russian cargo cheap but distant.
Sibur cut offers hard (HDPE Film -3,400, HDPE yarn -3,800 VND/kg vs 2 wks); domestic LDPE/LLDPE still falling.
paras: The Asian PE import market is fluctuating sharply this week as LLDPE futures on the Dalian Commodity Exchange (China) fell nearly 30 USD/ton over the past two days. In addition, Saudi Arabia's Sabic offering PE for July delivery at sharply lower prices than the previous month has made market sentiment more cautious. Many buyers are waiting to see where Sabic settles its July PE prices before deciding to trade; quite a few other distributors still hold the view that PE's price decline will continue into July, so they are buying only modest quantities.

Specifically, Sabic's bid price for LDPE Film for July 2026 delivery to Vietnam has just been announced at 1,300 USD/ton/CIF; the bid for LDPE coating is at 1,460 USD/ton/CIF; the bid for LLDPE Film products is at 1,110 USD/ton/CIF; the bid for LLDPE injection and roto products is at 1,250 USD/ton/CIF; mLLDPE C6 is at 1,280 USD/ton/CIF; the bid for HDPE Film is at 1,150-1,180 USD/ton/CIF; the bid for HDPE blow is at 1,180 USD/ton/CIF; and the bid for HDPE injection is at 1,150-1,200 USD/ton/CIF depending on grade... goods CIF from Singapore.

Regarding Chinese goods, Petrochina's settled selling price for LLDPE Film to Vietnamese buyers was recorded at 1,010-1,020 USD/ton/CIF, down 10 USD/ton from the settled price at the end of last week. Another distributor offered LLDPE Film MI 2 grades without additives from Chinese manufacturers to Vietnam at prices significantly lower than last week. Specifically: Baofeng's LLDPE Film 7042 was offered at 1,080 USD/ton/CIF – down 40 USD/ton from last week; Ningmei's LLDPE 7042 for July delivery was at 1,036 USD/ton/CIF; Yulong's LLDPE 7042 for July delivery was at 1,095 USD/ton/CIF; Kunlun's LLDPE 7042N was at 1,095 USD/ton/CIF – down sharply by 62 USD/ton from last week...

Today, June 24, a distributor of China's STL offered HDPE Film FS7000 to Vietnam at 1,200 USD/ton/CIF, down 30 USD/ton from last week. Nevertheless, this price is considered still high. According to the distributor, it is almost impossible to close a deal, because the HDPE Film of BASF and Sinopec China is selling at 1,160-1,170 USD/ton/CIF.

US-origin PE offered to Vietnam is also quite plentiful and cheap; however, the goods are for distant delivery and many distributors offer goods repackaged by traders themselves, with unclear grades and manufacturers, so buyers are all cautious. For example, one distributor offered US-origin LLDPE Film at 900 USD/ton/CIF, HDPE Film at 930-950 USD/ton/CIF, and US-origin HDPE blow at 960-980 USD/ton/CIF... Some buyers think these may be near-prime or off-grade goods...

Regarding Russian goods, the distributor of Russia's Sibur this week offered many PE products to Vietnamese buyers, with goods at Hai Phong port, quoted in VND. Offer prices fell significantly compared to two weeks ago. Accordingly, Sibur offered LLDPE Film MI 2, with additives, at 1,100 USD/ton/CFR Hai Phong; it offered this product in VND at 32,000 VND/kg/ incl. VAT, down 2,200 VND/kg from two weeks ago.

The mLLDPE offer is at 33,500 VND/kg/ incl. VAT, down 2,300 VND/kg from two weeks ago. The offer for HDPE Film products is at 33,100 VND/kg/ incl. VAT, down sharply 3,400 VND/kg from two weeks ago; the HDPE yarn offer is at 31,800 VND/kg/ incl. VAT, down 3,800 VND/kg after two weeks; the HDPE blow offer is at 34,400 VND/kg/ incl. VAT, down 1,800 VND/kg; while the HDPE injection offer is at 33,700-34,000 VND/kg/ incl. VAT, down 1,500 VND/kg from two weeks ago... In addition, Sibur also offered some near-prime and off-grade products. Customers buying large quantities of 4 containers or more will receive additional new promotional policies.

Domestically, PE's downward price trend has not yet shown signs of stopping; in fact, many products continue to fall quickly. Accordingly, at the start of this week, Chinese-origin LDPE Film was commonly offered at around 43,000 VND/kg/ incl. VAT; Middle Eastern-origin LDPE Film such as Sabic, Tasnee, and Borouge was commonly offered at around 44,000 VND/kg/ incl. VAT; Lotte Titan Malaysia-origin LDPE Film 260GG was offered at retail at 34,500 VND/kg/ incl. VAT... down about 1,000 VND/kg from the end of last week.

For LLDPE, the LLDPE Film offer price today was commonly recorded in the range of 33,000-34,500 VND/kg/ incl. VAT. Notably, a large distributor offered Sabic's LLDPE Film 218WJ at 33,500 VND/kg/ incl. VAT at Hai Phong port; some traders offered at 34,500 VND/kg/ incl. VAT to retail customers around the Hanoi area. In the low-price segment, China's LLDPE 7042/7042N was offered at 33,000 VND/kg/ incl. VAT but recorded transaction prices of only around 32,000 VND/kg/ incl. VAT... This new price level is down 500-1,000 VND/kg from last week, depending on grade and supplier.

HDPE is the product that has held its price more firmly than LDPE and LLDPE over the past few weeks. A fairly large distributor in the north offered Qamar's HDPE Film F1 at 36,000 VND/kg/ excl. VAT, and offered it at 38,000 VND/kg/ incl. VAT. The spot inventory of this product at traders' warehouses is not large. Another product that also has little spot inventory and holds a high price is PTT Thailand's HDPE yarn 5000S, recorded as offered by a supplier at 39,000 VND/kg/ incl. VAT in the south and 39,300 VND/kg/ incl. VAT in the central region...

summary: Sabic's S-Oil Korean-JV July bids dropped very sharply (yarn/inj 1,040, copo 1,080 CIF).
Korean flood continues — Hanwha HJ500 990 CIF (deal at 970), Lotte 1,030-1,040 — undercutting Chinese.
Hyosung Vina cut yarn another 500 to 34,700, still too high to sell; NSRP 114G deals to 32,000.
paras: Yesterday, June 23, the distributor of Saudi Arabia's Sabic announced bid prices for PP resin produced at the joint-venture plant in South Korea (S-Oil) for delivery to Vietnam, with the shipment to be delivered at the end of July 2026. This month's bid for PP yarn/injection is at 1,040 USD/ton/CIF and the bid for PP copo is at 1,080 USD/ton/CIF..., down very sharply from the offer prices at the end of last month.

From the beginning of June until now, the volume of PP offers from South Korea to Vietnam has been gradually increasing and offer prices have fallen quickly, competing more strongly with Chinese goods. Before S-Oil, the settled selling price of PP from Lotte Hyundai (South Korea) to Vietnam was also recorded at 1,030-1,040 USD/ton/CIF. Notably, earlier this week, a distributor offered PP injection HJ500 of South Korea's Hanwha Total to Vietnam at 990 USD/ton/CIF; the offer for Hanwha's PP block copo BJ300 was at 1,020 USD/ton/CIF. There is news that some buyers received prices that could close a deal for the above-mentioned PP HJ500 at 970 USD/ton/CIF for a quantity of 198 tons...

Domestically, a trader offered PP HJ500 at only 30,000 VND/kg/ incl. VAT for a shipment arriving in the country in early July, accepting further negotiation. This price is about 2,000 VND/kg cheaper than PP homo of Vietnam's NSRP origin, Chinese goods, or Russian goods. Hanwha's PP copo BJ300, BJ350, and BJ550 were also recorded as offered at around 33,000-34,000 VND/kg/ incl. VAT, lower than the domestic copo offer prices of Hyosung or other origins.

Regarding Chinese goods, the volume of import offers from suppliers remains quite sparse and scattered, not as active as 2-3 weeks ago. Today, a distributor offered Petrochina's PP yarn L5E89 to Vietnam at 1,105 USD/ton/CIF – down 50 USD/ton from last week; however, some buyers received Petrochina's PP yarn offer at 1,075 USD/ton/CIF, showing that offer prices among different suppliers vary quite widely... Domestically in our country, Chinese-origin PP homo is commonly offered at 32,000-34,000 VND/kg/ incl. VAT depending on the product, origin, and supplier.

For domestic goods, earlier this week, NSRP's offer price for PP 114G was lowest at 32,500 VND/kg/ incl. VAT; the lowest settled transaction price was at 32,000-32,200 VND/kg/ incl. VAT depending on quantity. This price is down 300-500 VND/kg from last week.

In the south, the Hyosung Vina plant has lowered its PP yarn offer price by a further 500 VND/kg from the end of last week, down to 34,700 VND/kg/ incl. VAT in the south. Today, Hyosung Vina quoted PP injection J700N at 32,000 VND/kg/ excl. VAT (with full 8% VAT, around 34,560 VND/kg/ incl. VAT); offered PP block copo J640N at 35,000 VND/kg/ excl. VAT (with full 8% VAT, around 37,800 VND/kg/ incl. VAT); offered PP random R701N at 43,000 VND/kg/ excl. VAT (with full 8% VAT, around 46,440 VND/kg/ incl. VAT)...
Nevertheless, this price for PP homo is still considered too high and difficult to sell domestically.

summary: Southern traders cut AGC Vina PM66R very sharply to 20,900-21,000 incl VAT (~1,000 below plant offer).
Coal-based Yihua SG-5 cut to 20,500 incl VAT (-500-700 on week); talk of base prices nearing 20,000 next month.
Oil-based offered 22,000-22,500 with slow trade; Shintech US far-delivery cargo at 670 CIF (Oct arrival).
paras: Today, June 24, some traders in the south lowered their offer price for PVC PM66R of domestic manufacturer AGC Vina (ACVN) very sharply to 20,900-21,000 VND/kg/ incl. VAT, about 1,000 VND/kg lower than the offer the plant itself issued in mid-June (the plant quoted 20,400-20,600 VND/kg/ excl. VAT).

With domestic plant PVC goods falling sharply, the downward price pressure on the PVC market in general is also greater. Some southern market participants believe that the base PVC price of the two domestic manufacturers early next month could reach the 20,000 VND/kg/ incl. VAT mark.

In the north, today, a large distributor lowered its offer for PVC SG-5 (coal-based) of Chinese origin from Yihua sharply down to 20,500 VND/kg/ incl. VAT, applicable to customers buying containers, with delivery at Yen Vien or Dong Anh station (Hanoi). This price is down a further 500-700 VND/kg from the beginning of the week.
Also in the north, many oil-based PVC products are being offered around 22,000-22,500 VND/kg/ incl. VAT, with quite slow trading.

Regarding imports, earlier this week, a distributor announced the selling price of PVC SE950/SE1000 of US-based Shintech to Vietnamese buyers at 670 USD/ton/CIF, distant delivery, expected to arrive in the country in October 2026. Previously, in mid-month, Shintech's settled PVC selling price for a shipment expected to arrive in September was at 690 USD/ton/CIF. Thus, this week's offer price has fallen a further 20 USD/ton.
